source: Kale VP et al. 2022 Toxicologic Pathology; Allison DB et al. 1999 Am J Psychiatry; Newcomer JW 2005 CNS Drugs; Reynolds GP & Kirk SL 2010 Pharmacol Ther
description: Expected findings for atypical antipsychotics (olanzapine, clozapine, risperidone, quetiapine). Class effects driven by dopaminergic + serotonergic + histaminergic + muscarinic receptor blockade in CNS, plus peripheral receptor binding. Metabolic syndrome (BW gain + dyslipidemia + impaired glucose tolerance) is regulatorily adverse despite the ostensibly desired CNS pharmacology (cross-references compound-class-flags.json:antipsychotic).

expected_findings.description: Body-weight gain from metabolic syndrome induction
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Sustained gain with concurrent dyslipidemia = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Histamine H1 + serotonin 5-HT2C + muscarinic M3 antagonism drive hyperphagia and altered glucose homeostasis. Comprehensive meta-analysis in Allison 1999 documented across all atypical antipsychotics.

Histamine H1 antagonism in hypothalamic centers + 5-HT2C antagonism increases appetite. Cross-references C7 corroboration trigger BW_up_same_dose_sex via compound_class:antipsychotic in endpoint-adverse-direction.json.

Olanzapine and clozapine carry FDA black-box warnings for hyperglycemia / new-onset diabetes (Newcomer 2005). Mechanism includes direct beta-cell effect + insulin resistance from adipose accumulation.
Class effect documented across atypical antipsychotics (Reynolds & Kirk 2010); olanzapine and clozapine show strongest signal.
Component of metabolic syndrome induction; pairs with triglyceride elevation.

expected_findings.description: Sedation from H1 antagonism + central D2 blockade
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Profound or persistent sedation interfering with feeding/grooming = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Expected on-target CNS effect; severity scales with dose.
Muscarinic M4 partial agonism + alpha-adrenergic effects drive salivation; particularly prominent for clozapine and olanzapine.
